# Reichenau Memorial Website Project Instructions

This is a memorial website dedicated to the victims of the Arbeitserziehungslager Reichenau (Labor Education Camp Reichenau), which operated from 1941 to 1945 in Innsbruck, Austria.

## Project Purpose
- Memorial website for Nazi labor camp victims
- Educational resource about historical persecution
- Documentation of new memorial installation
- Digital archive and teaching platform

## Project Structure
- `index.html` - Main HTML file with semantic structure
- `css/styles.css` - Respectful, modern CSS with mobile-first approach
- `js/script.js` - Interactive JavaScript with accessibility features
- `images/` - Directory for memorial and historical images (see images/README.md)

## Code Style Guidelines
- Use semantic HTML5 elements appropriate for memorial content
- Follow mobile-first responsive design principles
- Maintain accessibility standards (WCAG 2.1) - crucial for educational sites
- Use modern CSS features (Grid, Flexbox, Custom Properties)
- Write clean, well-commented code
- Follow progressive enhancement principles
- Ensure respectful and dignified presentation of content

## Design Principles
- **Respectful Design**: Appropriate styling for memorial and educational content
- **Color scheme**: Subdued green theme (#2c5530 primary) reflecting dignity and remembrance
- **Typography**: Graphik and Untitled Serif fonts for readability and gravitas
- **Smooth interactions**: Subtle animations appropriate for memorial context
- **Accessibility**: Essential for educational institutions and diverse audiences
- **Performance**: Fast loading for global educational access

## Content Guidelines
- **Historical Accuracy**: All content must be factually accurate and well-researched
- **Respectful Tone**: Dignified language appropriate for memorial context
- **Educational Focus**: Content should serve educational and remembrance purposes
- **Victim-Centered**: Honor the memory of those who suffered and died
- **International Perspective**: Consider multilingual accessibility

## Technical Features
- Responsive navigation with clear structure
- Smooth scrolling between educational sections
- Form validation for educational inquiries
- Accessibility features for diverse learners
- Foundation for multilingual support

## Important Considerations
- This is a memorial site - all changes should respect the dignity of victims
- Content deals with sensitive historical topics requiring careful handling
- Educational institutions may be primary users - ensure accessibility
- International audience requires clear, accessible language
- Mobile optimization important for field trips and on-site visits
